checks.a_analysis: I have carefully compared the text in the output JSON with the source PDF. The headings, table content (where provided), and paragraph text all have a direct basis in the source document. I found no instances of fabricated body text, section headings, or other invented content.

The output contains a URL in the Symantec training section (https://www.symantec.com/services/education-services/training-courses) which I verified exists exactly as written on page 3 of the source PDF. No other contact information such as phone numbers or email addresses were found or fabricated.

A significant portion of the document is missing from the structured output. Specifically, the final two pages of the source document are entirely absent. Page 18 of 19 ('SHI (CowdStrike) Price List') and Page 19 of 19 ('PRESIDIO (CISCO) Price List') are nowhere to be found in the JSON sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document contains 'SHI (CowdStrike) Price List' on page 18 of 19 and 'PRESIDIO (CISCO) Price List' on page 19 of 19.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON sections end with the 'CROWDSTRIKE' section and do not include the CowdStrike or Cisco price lists.

checks.a_analysis: There are major omissions in several tables. 1. The 'Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List Continued' table in the JSON contains only 1 data row (Line 10), whereas the corresponding table on page 14 of 19 in the source contains 20 data rows (Lines 10-29). 2. The 'CrowdStrike Cost Proposal Form' table in the JSON contains only header information and 0 data rows, whereas the source on page 16 of 19 contains approximately 9 data rows detailing cost items.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source page 14 of 19 contains lines 10-29 in the table; page 16 of 19 contains data rows for 'Cloud Subscription/Licensing Fee', 'Customization...', etc.
checks.b_evidence_output: The Trellix table contains only line 10; the CrowdStrike table contains only headers.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 3 and 4, corresponding to source pages 14 and 16.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Large sections of the source's content are not captured in the structured format (text/tables). The entire 'SOPHOS' price list (source pages 5-9 of 19) is represented only by images, with no corresponding table data or text. Similarly, the 'Carahsoft Trellix' list starting on page 13 of 19 is mostly ignored, appearing as images or severely truncated tables.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source pages 5-9 contain extensive tabular data for Sophos products and services.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'SOPHOS' section in the JSON contains only images and no structured data elements representing the content of those tables.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: I inspected the text that was successfully converted (such as the Symantec and Trellix Professional Services details) and found that the wording and numerical values match the source document accurately. No semantic-altering minor differences were detected.

checks.a_analysis: The output contains two identical 'Professional Services' sections (headings 10 and 11) within the Trellix section. However, upon inspection of the source PDF, this duplication exists in the original document (pages 11 and 12 are near-duplicates of this specific content). As the conversion faithfully follows the source's own repetition, it is not considered a defect.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent. Throughout the document, price list titles for specific vendors are consistently assigned as H3 (e.g., headings 2, 3, 8, 12, 14). However, the continued section for the Trellix price list (heading 13) is promoted to H2. This is a regression in the hierarchy, as a 'Continued' heading should be at the same level as (or lower than) the heading it continues.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 14 title: "Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List (Continued)"

checks.a_analysis: Several tables suffer from severe structural corruption or truncation. The 'Carahsoft Trellix' table following heading 13 (Page 14) is truncated after the first data row (Line 10), omitting lines 11 through 29. Furthermore, the content of Page 15 (Lines 30-43) is entirely missing from the section. Additionally, the 'CrowdStrike Cost Proposal Form' table (heading 15, Page 16) contains only the header row and omits all 9 data rows present in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 14 table (Lines 10-29); Page 15 table (Lines 30-43); Page 16 table (9 data rows)
checks.b_evidence_output: The table under heading-13 contains only Line 10; heading-15 table contains only the header row; p15 data is absent.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the existing elements follows the logical sequence of the source document. While there is a significant amount of missing content (truncation of tables and omission of the final three pages of the document), the elements present maintain their relative positions.
The unordered list under 'Professional Services' (Page 11/12) is correctly identified and structured in the JSON output, preserving the four bullet points.

The link found in the Broadcom table (Page 3) is correctly extracted and formatted. While links embedded in pages converted solely as images (like the Sophos section) are not interactive, the conversion of text-based URLs in other sections is handled correctly without fabrication.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully represents the visual organization of the source PDF. The document structure, including the sequence of reseller price lists (Symantec, Sophos, Trellix, CrowdStrike, and Cisco), is maintained. The transition from complex multi-column tables in the PDF to a responsive single-column layout in HTML is handled well, preserving the logical flow and hierarchy of information. Headings and tables appear in the expected order corresponding to the source pages.

The tables visible in the HTML screenshot (e.g., Symantec Price List, Professional Services, and a portion of the Trellix Price List) are correctly rendered as structured HTML tables. Headers are distinct from body rows, and column alignment is maintained (ITEM #, QTY, UNIT, DESCRIPTION, COST). Data within cells is readable and not overlapping. For instance, the Symantec table accurately reproduces all columns and their respective contents from PDF page 3. The additional specification rows below the main table on PDF page 3 are also correctly organized into a two-column table format in the HTML.

The source PDF is a text-based price list document consisting entirely of headings, tables, and text blocks. There are no meaningful images, charts, diagrams, or logos present in the source PDF. Therefore, no images are expected in the HTML output, and none are missing.

There is no content duplication introduced by the conversion process. While the 'Professional Services' section under Trellix (McAfee) appears twice in the HTML body, this is a faithful reproduction of the source PDF, where the same block appears on both page 11 (at the bottom) and page 12 (as the only content). The Table of Contents correctly lists repeating headings that appear on multiple pages of the source (e.g., 'SHI (Sophos) Price List (Continued)'), which is expected navigation design and not content duplication.
